body.title: [triage] loguru sink raises ValueError: I/O operation on closed file at teardown (+2 collection errors) — the logger outlives the stream it logs to.
body.intention: I intend that the logging path never raises — a logger that throws during teardown destroys the record of the very shutdown it was there to witness. [intention_date: 2026-07-14]
body.description:
**OBSERVED 2026-07-14** in the full-suite run under the tester (753s, 7781 passed / 314 failed / 2 errors):

    --- Logging error ---
    File ".venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/loguru/_simple_sinks.py", line 16, in write
        self._stream.write(message)
    ValueError: I/O operation on closed file.
    --- End of logging error ---

A loguru sink is still bound to a stream that has already been closed — something logs during teardown
(or from a daemon thread outliving the fixture that opened the sink). The 2 collection `errors` in the same
run may or may not be the same cause; that is unmeasured and must not be assumed.

**WHY IT MATTERS BEYOND NOISE:** the failure is in the LOGGING PATH ITSELF. CLAUDE.md requires a log at
every state change and every interface crossing precisely so that a boundary bug is visible. A sink that
throws at shutdown is a hole in exactly the record we would need to diagnose a shutdown bug — and it
announces itself as a stack trace in the test output, where it is easy to learn to ignore.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/diagnostic_base/* (the base JSON sink), tests/conftest.py (fixture
teardown order), unseen_university/shim.py (ShimLoopThread — a daemon thread logging after its owner is gone
is the likeliest cause and should be checked FIRST).

**Scope boundary:** stop the logging path from raising. Do not restructure the sink hierarchy.

**Completion criteria:** a full suite run produces zero 'Logging error' blocks; the 2 collection errors are
separately identified and either fixed or ticketed by name (they are NOT assumed to be the same bug).

**Test plan:** red->green — a test that opens a device sink, closes the stream, and logs from the shim's
loop thread. RED: ValueError. GREEN: the record is dropped or rerouted, and nothing raises. The hollow build
this must fail: wrapping the sink's write in a bare `except: pass` — that silences the symptom and deletes
the evidence, and CLAUDE.md's audit already forbids a silent except.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
